content: In summary I think making a change like that would make for a repetitive reading experience, as I feel like "Lemuria" would be put in too many more places than it should be.
to be honest, I do very much agree with everything you said, but the decision-making authority is out of our hands currently
Then I'll refer back to [this write-up](https://discord.com/channels/1402537664619479100/1407785080025383157), state that I've said my peace, and leave it to the decision-makers.
Ace has to sign off on major term changes, I did ping her a few times but I haven't heard anything back yet so I'm not sure

Idk if this counts as a major term change though? It seems like it's a general term that got translated into a few different equally valid words and it's not really something that feels (at least to me) like it needs fixing

Yeah I agree the term itself, as a rarely mentioned term when talking about “that” underwater city, offers some estrangement upon encountering. While the “spiral” and “depths” meaning within the word offer extra space for interpretation
My point is the current term might variant too much for me that the connection of it’s appearance and Lemuria/submerged kingdom might be somewhat loose
It will be totally fine to keep the name of Murphy’s skill as it’s usage of “螺湮” seems to adapt more of the vortex meaning than the Lemuria meaning
But I remain doubt in other places, and however it is up to Ace for the term changes
yeah on further thought it doesn't make sense to change murphy's card name
螺湮呼唤 is an enemy Seafarer attack so that can probably be fine to use lemuria

This (nuance) is the point I was hoping to get across, but I'm very tired today and also hungry so I'm not at 100%

I agree that any use of that word 螺湮 should be seen as an *opportunity* to use "Lemuria" but ONLY if it makes significantly more sense than any other available word.
....I was trying to figure out where the other two came from but uhh
those are gmurphy cards
no wonder I couldn't find them
that.....should probably be a direct lemuria reference, I think
also I wonder if the NDA is coming soon because hmm, I don't think we're supposed to have access to this without an NDA

I’m going to sleep soon, so above are only my limited thoughts on this
Actually I encountered this issue when working on Sanga’s awakener story. There it refers directly to Lemuria, so I got a bit confused when looked upon the terms and saw many spirals…
Seems we have to wait for Ace on this now whatever
per Ace
so we can probably add that to the list of stuff to do at some point
Also where was that posted?
Sweet. Yeah I just get so overwhelmed by big servers that I flatly mute pretty much everything

Fellas. Somehow this has not ever actually bothered me, but in the big standardization situation, if unchanged this kind of effect would be the only example (that I know of) of the usage of the term `DMG` to refer to anything but damage being dealt.
Personally, I don't see any issue with rewording this to "<:warn:1408311425952387122>Tentacle STR".

I'm definitely fine with the bar above the party's head that shows number of tentacles and how much damage they're about to deal being referred to as Tentacle DMG. I'm absolutely fine with that "Stat" being used in descriptions when referring *specifically* to that entire number, like in the case of Abyss Order using it as a number to modify calculated DMG with.
But effects that permanently or temporarily *increase* that number by a flat amount is *literally the exact mechanic* called STR.

Thoughts?

man, we really need to start putting a space (` `) after each "append this to a card" keyword tag.
While I'm at it I'd like to put a newline (`/n`) at the end of every card's description so that Exhaust, Retain, Void, etc can be separate from the rest of the text.
i get where you're coming from but ironically enough i feel like it'd actually been less helpful to separate the terms than to just use the same term for everything
tentacle dmg doesn't have a separate formula or interact weirdly with tentacle base damage like str does for normal damage
all tentacle dmg is the same stat so if you separate buffs out into tentacle str it becomes kinda like, hey why did they make this distinction does it behave differently from tentacle dmg
and people are probably going to get confused
on the flip side "gain 42 tentacle dmg" isn't exactly prime grammar but the meaning is quite clear and nobody's ever really gonna get confused as to what it means
I agree, I really wasn't attached to the idea at all. It's just adding confusion for the sake of standardization
I am 10000000000% behind this, i have always and will always be a huge supporter of using line breaks as organizational tools for text
imo all tags (innate, retain, exhaust, prepare, void) should be separated into a new line at the very bottom of a card

preferably in a very specific order though i suppose that probably isn't too important (and also not under our control since i presume the code handles appending text to tags added vs ingame mechanics)

i am ticked with that.. 
bla bla "p
repare bla bla
why is the P up there

```
            Blood
        of
Coition                 
repare.Gain 100 Keyflare.
```
i love how "of" is sitting in the middle alone
no spaces to break on + poor wordwrap ?

**Coition.⚠️Retain.Innate.Prepare.Gain** 100 Keyflare
Seems to have zero spaces, and apparently breaking between a period and a letter isn't a thing here
Hi! We will have to keep it as Tentacle DMG as the og text states it as DMG/伤害 not STR/力量;
playing it safe here
<:jenkin_processing:1408324289115062333>  do we have a singular name for the Rai Lai Lye etc issue or is it still WIP?
Rye
per the terms sheet https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QW8qr1y7DLFnTZVOHUTmw_IBL9bq8JEaIPKQGMOjzKE/edit?gid=1786279439#gid=1786279439
think this came up once in salvador's story so i'll make sure to add this

Question: I have noticed that for the same phrase “螺湮” in Chinese has different translations in different circumstances (according to the pic from term base). As the term itself seems to be a rather vague reference to depths/spirals/sea etc., I wonder if it’s necessary to work out a word/phrase in EN texts for “螺湮”, or we can freely use different terms according to the specific texts?
Since it seems to be a frequent term in texts related to Aequor characters, I think maybe Morimens intended to develop it as one of the game’s ingredients/styles…

every instance of it I've seen so far has been in some form of context relating to Lemuria (except maybe Murphy's skill card Vortex Reversed, but I think that might still be a reference to Lemuria because of her story....)
have you found any instances of it that aren't related in some way to Lemuria or Seafarers?

I agree, as far as I can see it always related to Lemuria
maybe we can just make EN texts hint towards this direction too?
yep, makes sense to me
I have updated the terms sheet with suggestions:
if Ace approves then we can change it (not sure what the timeline on that is though, I haven't heard anything from Ace yet regarding terms changes)

Idk how I feel about just replacing that term with "Lemuria" wholesale.
Does that word (螺湮) specifically mean Lemuria? Is that the word used in place of every mention of the city/kingdom of Lemuria?
Cus if so that's fine, but even then some of those names just feel... Off. Like "Lemuria Reversed".
If we are going with this I think at least we should call that last one "Lemurian Waltz"
it's not the actual name for lemuria (利莫里亚), but refers to lemuria (it's made up of the words 螺, spiral, and 湮, submerged/depths)
it's a bit weird because it's used to refer to Lemuria quite often but it's like, Abyss
it's like, tulu's title is Lord of the Abyss but abyss here is lemuria
I don't really have any more elegant way of translating it but Ace has it as Lemuria in the sheet so I just went with that
any other decisions would probably need Ace's feedback...
I find that fairly functional at least in English then, I wouldn't change anything.

If it's not referring specifically to Lemuria, but rather the area or vibe in which Lemuria "exists" then we should use whatever interesting descriptor fits the context, instead of trying to shove Lemuria in everywhere there could be other words.

Lorewise “螺湮” is a variant translation of “拉莱耶” (the more common translation of R’lyeh in CN)
so out of the game it can be seen as a direct reference to “that” city underwater 
I think it’s better to make the in-game connection of it with Lemuria more closer… reading vortex in one place and spiral in another might cause some distraction

I disagree, if it's not blatantly obvious that the word used should very specifically be "Lemuria" and having any other word would make the sentence or statement awkward, then we should use whatever the most flavorful descriptor is.

At no point was it not clear to me that these terms were referring to the mystical deep sea, in particular a specific part of the mystical deep sea, that being Lemuria
It feels to me like replacing every instance of "Kingdom" with "Lemuria" just because it's the only Kingdom we talk about in the game.
